jobs.description:
It's Day 1 for Amazon's next billion dollar business and we are seeking a skilled Senior Financial Analyst to join the Leo Procurement Finance team, playing a pivotal role in optimizing financial planning and analysis for Electrical Mechanical Systems (EMS) procurement operations. In this role, you will provide critical financial guidance and analysis that drives strategic decision-making for one of Leo's most complex and capital-intensive supply chain areas. You will build comprehensive financial models, create scalable processes, and deliver impactful insights that support procurement strategies for satellite avionics, electrical power systems, battery systems, and mechanical subsystems that power Leo's satellite technology. Your analysis will directly inform procurement decisions and influence investment strategies at the highest levels of the organization.

This exciting opportunity allows you to create foundational capabilities for a rapidly scaling business that will shape procurement and supply chain investment strategies within Leo Finance. You'll partner closely with EMS procurement leadership to analyze supplier agreements, build scalable procurement processes, and design reporting frameworks to communicate results to Leo leadership. If you're passionate about transforming financial data into actionable business insights and want to make a meaningful impact in a growing team, this role offers exceptional visibility and growth potential.

This position offers a unique opportunity to make significant impact as you help shape a growing, global business line. If you thrive in a dynamic environment where your financial acumen translates directly into operational excellence and cost optimization, this role is perfect for you!


Key job responsibilities
• Design, build, and maintain comprehensive financial models that evaluate EMS procurement strategies, supplier performance, and enable data-driven decision making for multi-million dollar procurement decisions across avionics, electrical power, battery, and mechanical systems.
• Develop detailed cost forecasts and budget plans for EMS procurement that inform Leo's satellite production roadmap and identify opportunities for cost optimization and efficiency gains.
• Partner with EMS procurement stakeholders to analyze and influence supplier agreements, contract structures, and pricing models, drafting written financial summaries for Leo leadership review.
• Develop and implement scalable reporting mechanisms that provide visibility into key procurement performance indicators including purchase order tracking, inventory turnover, supplier performance metrics, and cost variance analysis for executive leadership.


A day in the life
Your day starts with reviewing key procurement financial metrics and preparing for a meeting with EMS procurement partners to evaluate the financial impact of a proposed supplier agreement for satellite avionics components. You spend time analyzing contract structures, building financial models to assess total cost of ownership, and forecasting potential cost savings opportunities. You draft a financial summary on a proposed multi-year EMS supply agreement, calling out unit economics, volume commitments, payment terms, and any supply chain risks.
jobs.description_short: It's Day 1 for Amazon's next billion dollar business and we are seeking a skilled Senior Financial Analyst to join the Leo Procurement Finance team, playing a pivotal role in optimizing financial planning

jobs.description:
We invent bold solutions in sustainable transportation and returns experiences which create trust and value for customers, selling partners, and shippers that benefit people, planet, and profit. We champion and lead the way to achieve Zero Defects, Zero Cost of Returns, Zero Product Waste and Zero Carbon, at scale.

Worldwide Returns & ReCommerce enhances the relationship with Amazon’s customers through Support Options and a Returns process that is simple, transparent, and convenient. We work to reduce returns by helping customers keep what they bought and removing defects that would cause a return (Zero Defects). When items are returned or damaged, we maximize reuse and recovery to achieve Zero Cost of Returns and Zero Product Waste, benefiting people, planet, and profit.

The WWR&R Finance team seeks a self-driven Finance Manager excited about solving complex problems that have a customer facing impact, require strategic long-term thinking and help drive the 4 zeroes.

As the Finance Manager, you will partner directly with business, tech and finance teams to identify the right business strategies, help define roadmaps, and provide guidance and reporting. You will play a critical role in communicating business strategy and results with company leadership up to the SVP level. You will own a product that supports a Senior Executive Team member's goal, and help build a robust product that is error-free, can scale and is reliable. As a key member of the team, you will influence business model, long term vision and decisions that will substantially reduce cost to sere customers. You will have a strong operational and controllership mindset as we continue our mission to build best-in-class reverse logistics processes.

The successful candidate has demonstrated the following abilities: they influence business owners, support thoughtful decision-making, and adapt to a rapidly-evolving environment. They collaborate with senior executives and various business units across Amazon. They bring excellent verbal and written communication skills. A successful candidate is an effective multitasker, adept at identifying high-priority work and projects that maximize results for key stakeholders, and excited about solving complex problems that affect company-wide strategic decisions.



Key job responsibilities
- Own definitions, automation roadmap and data integrity for the NCRC P&L, which is used to plan and report for an S-Team goal
- Use solid financial knowledge and business acumen to build financial models, interpret data, draw conclusions, and make recommendations that deliver results, drive improvements, and influence leaders throughout Amazon. These decisions can have global impact.
- Uses SQL, financial systems and advanced MS Excel functions to work with large data sets. Outputs plans, forecasts, budgets, and consolidations.
- Manages roadmap for product and prioritizes feature development based on impact
- Creates effective business partnerships, manages competing priorities, and provides guidance based on data analysis during unexpected events.
- Uses active listening skills intuitively to communicate data (both written and verbal) in a clear, concise, and unambiguous manner. Adjusts style for various audiences to articulate complex finance issues clearly.
- Collects and analyzes quantitative data to develop relevant reports that drive cost reductions.
- Participates in hiring, developing, and mentoring peers and team members. Seeks and leverages diverse perspectives in all activities.
- Identifies opportunities to resolve defects proactively. Improves, scales, or simplifies finance tools across teams and organizations.
- Contributes to work life harmony by being a positive, collaborative, and effective influencer to new team members as an indicator of operations excellence.


About the team
The WWRR finance team works on high visibility, complex deliverables that have a huge impact on Amazon's bottom-line. We partner with senior stakeholders across WWRR as well as partner teams. It will be a challenging role with lots of opportunities to learn and grow. You will be part of a close-knit team that supports each other and learns together.

jobs.description:
Amazon is seeking an analytical, strategic, and self-directed business development leader to drive AI initiatives, projects, and partnerships within Corporate Business Development (CorpBD). CorpBD is part of a centralized Corporate Development team and is responsible for generating, managing, and executing some of Amazon’s most complex and highest impact partnership engagements. This includes managing relationships and negotiations which may be non-standard and highly ambiguous in nature, in need of a centralized advocate, and/or have broader implications to multiple Amazon business teams. You will be part of a BD team that supports strategic deal and thought partnership for Amazon's AI efforts, including AGI's Nova family of models. You should be forward-thinking and have experiencing in negotiating complex deal terms in a space that is rapidly evolving.

This is a key role that is highly visible at the most senior levels of Amazon, and you will need to work collaboratively with internal teams (primarily AGI) to identify emerging use-cases, support and expand key partnerships, and align roadmaps as well as thoughtfully structure partnerships with possible derivative investments, while staying current on emerging trends in AI. We dive deep and provide thoughtful analysis, but are curious, adaptable and action-oriented, focused on quickly gaining enough context to enable informed decision-making.

Ideal candidates have an entrepreneurial spirit, experience incubating new programs and bringing them to scale, and strong cross-functional skills to run programs across a diverse set of businesses, industries, and teams. Candidates must be willing to roll up their sleeves to solve complex problems by working effectively with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced setting that is constantly evolving while simultaneously being a leader and pacesetter. They will have the ability to drive due diligence, identify key tension points/tradeoffs, spearhead term sheet and deal negotiations, and create and present strategic documents and transaction recommendations.

A day in the life
A day in the life
- Act as a subject matter expert on AI
- Proactively identify opportunities with emerging companies to support AI development efforts
- Evaluate opportunities, identify risks, and lead transaction diligence efforts and execution
- Manage and seek ways to expand relationships with existing strategic partners
- Collaborate with internal Amazon teams to identify emerging use-cases and pursue partnerships that could accelerate our GTM efforts
- Work across stakeholder teams who advise and support decisions and deals

jobs.title: Senior Corporate BD Manager, Corporate Business Development

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Management, Business Administration, Economics, Engineering, or Marketing
- 7+ years of experience in enterprise sales, business development, or account management, preferably in telecommunications, satellite, connectivity, or technology sectors
- Proven track record of closing complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise deals with deal sizes of $1M+
- Experience managing full sales lifecycle from prospecting through contract negotiation and closure
jobs.description:
Amazon Leo is an initiative to launch a constellation of Low Earth Orbit satellites that will provide low-latency, high-speed broadband connectivity to unserved and underserved communities around the world.

Amazon Leo is looking for experienced, self-starter Account Executive to drive adoption of Leo services with North American enterprise customers across multiple verticals (e.g. Energy, Manufacturing, Healthcare).

You are responsible for identifying, evaluating, negotiating, and managing complex deals with multiple key accounts. You are responsible for managing the lifecycle of complex cross-functional customers, delivering outcomes with cross-organizational impact. You execute and define sales strategies that complements product strategy with limited oversight from others. You not only implement but also develop strategy for getting and delivering customer feedback.

You serve as a partner or thought leader for other sales teams on opportunities to contribute to the company. You contribute to strategic documents including but not limited to regional and vertical GTM and account plans. You own reporting on forecasting and planning the business impact of each opportunity, with a focus on actuals vs. plan targets.

You can deeply impact measurable business metrics are able to close or significantly grow large or more complex medium sized business opportunities.


Key job responsibilities
Contribute to the build out of Amazon Leo service revenue with specific focus on key North American enterprise customers
Be customer obsessed. Think on behalf of the customer and work to meet the customer’s stated and unstated needs and aspirations
Execute strategic and tactical business development initiatives while working with key internal stakeholders (e.g., Amazon Leo product and engineering teams, legal, finance) and sales team colleagues
Be the internal coordinator supporting complex contract negotiations and complex business requirements and activities which deliver results
Adept at thinking strategically and analytically about business priorities, product, and technical challenges, with the ability to deliver target-based results working cross-organizationally

Export Control Requirement:
Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

jobs.title: Sr. Account Executive , Amazon Leo

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 1+ years of account management, project or program management or buying experience
- Bachelor's degree
- Experience using Excel and other business analytic tools
jobs.description:
Interested in working on an innovative and high-impact business that is changing the way customers think about grocery? Amazon is looking for a talented Category Manager (CM) responsible for setting the strategic direction for their business on Amazon Fresh and managing vendor relationships for their categories.

The ideal candidate possesses experience in buying, marketing, and/or finance, with a track record of successful relationship management, strong analytical acumen, and experience generating and managing reporting and analysis. Great communication skills and understanding of Excel will be essential to your success. The ideal candidate is a self-starter who will take ownership of projects and business problems to a successful result.


Key job responsibilities
Our environment is fast-paced, and requires someone who is flexible, detail-oriented, analytical, and comfortable working with multiple teams, vendors, partners, and management. On a day to day basis, you will:
- Develop long-term strategies and significantly influences cross-category plans
- Work cross functionally, both within Amazon and the strategic vendor, and implement scalable, simple and automated solutions to address customer pain points
- Create opportunities for growth, customer acquisition, and brand- building for multiple sub-categories
- Audit metrics regularly, determine and prioritize business opportunities, identify risks and make recommendations to pursue
- Identify industry trends and develop innovative marketing and merchandising initiatives to support growth
- Drive strategic negotiations with large vendors and represent Amazon when meeting with senior leaders

A day in the life
- Be the ‘business owner’ for your vendors and regions, working autonomously and possessing a complete understanding of internal and external variables that impact your vendors
- Coordinate cross-functional teams, and communicate with internal and external partners, while meeting tight deadlines
- Conduct financial analysis of business opportunities to meet and exceed revenue and profitability goals for your category
- Analyze weekly and monthly performance metrics and manage communication with internal and external stakeholders to hit financial targets
- Provide input for your category regarding promotional activity, space allocation, pricing, productivity analysis, and forecasting

About the team
This team is a highly collaborative team of category managers and risk managers that collaborate across three offices (Arlington, Seattle, and NYC). We problem solve together and individually (often with weekly brainstorming sessions and open forums where we ideate on solutions), identify and implement scalable solutions for root causes, and above all obsess over our customer and potential customers. We celebrate one another's successes and support each other through headwinds as we work towards our shared goal of growing this category.

jobs.description:
Application deadline: Mar 9, 2026

Have you ever wanted to be part of a team who is building industry changing technology? Amazon Leo is an initiative to launch a constellation of Low Earth Orbit satellites that will provide low-latency, high-speed broadband network connectivity to unserved and underserved communities around the world.

Do you want to work at a company where you are the owner, where you are encouraged to build, and where you have the autonomy to push boundaries? Do you have a passion for design and a desire to bring delightful, products and services to life in an entirely new way? This describes your daily journey. Are you ready? Then join us on Amazon Leo.

Amazon Leo is looking for experienced, self-starter Senior Sales Leader to drive adoption of Leo broadband and additional services with Mobile Network Operators in North America. This is an exciting opportunity to influence and implement the Amazon Leo sales strategy with strategic partners in North American. This position is located in Bellevue WA or Arlington VA and also open to candidates in or within reasonable distance of other Amazon corporate office locations.

This position may require access to information, technology, or hardware that is subject to export control laws and regulations, including the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) and the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR). Employment in this position is contingent upon obtaining any required export licenses or other approvals from the United States government. As such, the successful candidate must be eligible to obtain any necessary export licenses or approvals based on their nationality, citizenship, and any other factors considered by the applicable export control regulations.

Export Control Requirement:
Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

Key job responsibilities
• Influence strategy for Amazon Leo with specific focus on Mobile Network Operators in North America
• Curious to learn in depth about both technology and our customers
• A curious self-starter with a proven track record of diving into unknown territory, learning new concepts, and pushing your comfort zone.
• An effective multi-tasker who navigates a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment with grace and efficiency.
• Excellent at creating and maintaining working relationships across all levels and disciplines, receptive to feedback and input from others, and quick to apply changes.
• Work with internal subject matter experts to gather information, synthesize business and technical concepts, build strategic business metrics, and write original content that drives decision-making at the highest levels of the organization.
• Experience working in start-up mode building or supporting products and services from the ground up.
• Implement business development plan while working with key internal stakeholders (e.g., Amazon Leo product and engineering teams, legal, support)
• Manage complex contract negotiations and complex business requirements and activities which deliver results.
• Adept at thinking strategically and analytically about business, product, and technical challenges, with the ability to build and convey compelling value propositions, and work cross-organizationally to build consensus.
• Action-oriented, detailed programmatic approach to implement complex programs and strategic initiatives with customers
• Consistently exceeds key performance metrics and Delivers Results


A day in the life
About the team
Here at Amazon, we embrace our differences. We are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. Amazon has ten employee-led affinity groups, reaching 40,000 employees in over 190 chapters globally. We have innovative benefit offerings, and host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 16 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious and earn trust.

Work/Life Balance
Our team puts a high value on work-life balance. It isn’t about how many hours you spend at home or at work; it’s about the flow you establish that brings energy to both parts of your life. We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.

Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional and enable them to take on more complex tasks in the future.

Innovation is part of our DNA! Our goal is to be Earth's most customer-centric company, and we are just getting started. We need people who want to join an ambitious program that continues to push the state of the art in space based systems design and wireless systems.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience with CI/CD pipelines build processes
- Proficiency in one or more scripting languages (e.g., Python, Bash).
- Familiarity with test automation frameworks.
- Experience with data handling, validation, and transformation.

jobs.description:
Amazon’s project Leo is an initiative to launch a constellation of Low Earth Orbit satellites that will provide low-latency, high-speed broadband connectivity to unserved and underserved communities around the world.
Seller and Partner Products (SPP) team at Leo, works on initiatives that support Leo's Enterprise (B2B), and Channel Partners (B2B2X) customer segments to deliver streamlined sales and ordering processes. We are looking for a talented and experienced Systems Development Engineer with an action-oriented mindset and a focus on exceeding expectations.

Export Control Requirements:
Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

Key job responsibilities
CI/CD Pipeline Management - Designing and maintaining automated "pipelines" that build, test, and deploy code every time a developer makes a change.
Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Instead of manually configuring servers, you write code (using tools like AWS CDK) to automatically "spin up" and manage cloud environments.
System Monitoring & Observability: Setting up dashboards and alerts to track application health and proactively catch errors before users do.
Security: Integrating security checks directly into the automated pipeline to ensure code is scanned for vulnerabilities before it reaches production.
Test automation: Integrating test automation into automated pipeline.
Measuring and automating incident response.
Monitoring/reporting on pipeline issues and source control usage by the team.
Creating and maintaining DevOps SOPs for SPP development team.
Mentoring engineers on software engineering best practices

jobs.description:
At Zappos Tech, we're on a mission to deliver unparalleled shopping experiences to our customers. We're seeking a Software Development Engineer II (SDE2) to join our team at Zappos. As an SDE2, you will contribute significantly to software development of Zappos web properties and associated systems. Your work will span the entire customer journey, from Homepage to checkout: Search, Search Results, Homepage, Ads, Widgets, Recommendations, and much more. If you're passionate about innovative products, thrive in dynamic environments, and are ready to grow your technical and leadership skills, we would love to hear from you!

Key job responsibilities
- Develop and Maintain: Play a key role in the development and maintenance of Zappos web properties, and associated frameworks and systems.
- Collaborate for Success: Work closely with product managers, UX designers, and engineers. Your contributions will be crucial in creating optimal customer experiences and steering technology choices.
- Coach and Guide: Provide guidance and feedback to help maintain high coding standards and practices. Your efforts will contribute to the growth and development of our engineering team.
- Solve Problems: Address complex issues within a well-understood technology strategy. You'll balance business needs with technical solutions to ensure scalability and future growth.
- Uphold Best Practices: Your contributions will not only address problems efficiently but also adhere to and promote best practices within the team.
- Learn and Grow: You'll have opportunities for continuous learning and contributing to the team's strategy and goals, fostering an inclusive and growth-oriented environment.


A day in the life
You spend most of your time building software - including decomposing product requirements, writing technical design documents, building and testing new features, fixing issues, and improving system performance. Code reviews are part of your regular routine. You regularly use AI tooling as part of your day to day development tasks.

You might research new frameworks or tools, write technical documentation for complex features, or collaborate closely with teammates on challenging problems. Some days are deep focus on feature development, others involve mentoring or investigating production issues. The work varies but always centers on making Zappos better for our customers.


About the team
You'll join a tight-knit team of developers who take ownership of the entire customer experience on Zappos. Everyone here is passionate about creating seamless shopping experiences that make customers genuinely happy - whether that's perfecting the search flow, streamlining checkout, or building features that surprise and delight.

We work closely together, sharing knowledge across platforms and collaborating daily with product managers and engineers. The team values transparency, continuous learning, and taking initiative. You'll have the freedom to propose solutions, and experiment with new approaches to
improve the Zappos customer experience.

- 4+ years of tax, finance or a related analytical field experience

- 4+ years of creating process improvements with automation and analysis experience

jobs.description:
Join our dynamic team as a Senior Financial Analyst supporting marketing operations with financial insights. In this role, you'll partner directly with marketing leadership to drive strategic decision-making through sophisticated financial analysis and reporting. You'll be at the forefront of AI and automation initiatives that transform how our marketing organization operates, delivering productivity improvements and exceptional business outcomes.

This position offers a unique opportunity to apply your analytical expertise and problem-solving skills in a fast-paced environment where your work directly impacts business strategy. You'll build and maintain high-quality financial reporting systems, manage budget planning with rigorous standards, and identify opportunities to leverage emerging technologies to streamline processes and enhance decision-making capabilities.

Key job responsibilities
- Partner with marketing operations leadership to provide strategic financial guidance, build comprehensive budgeting frameworks, and assess return on investment for various marketing initiatives
- Develop and maintain sophisticated financial models and reporting systems that track performance metrics, headcount planning, and budget utilization to drive data-informed decision making
- Lead cross-functional automation and AI projects that enhance financial reporting capabilities and drive productivity improvements across the marketing organization
- Conduct detailed financial analysis to solve complex business challenges, delivering actionable insights that influence strategic direction and resource allocation
- Research and analyze financial, operational, and technical details to develop comprehensive business insights and effectively represent financial perspective in executive discussions

A day in the life
Your typical day involves collaborating closely with the marketing operations team to address strategic financial questions and provide analytical support. You'll build and refine high-quality reporting dashboards, prepare for and participate in cross-business reviews (xBRs), and respond to ad-hoc analysis requests from stakeholders. Throughout the day, you'll track budgets against plans, identify variances, and recommend course corrections. You'll also have opportunities to research and implement AI-driven solutions that enhance your team's financial planning capabilities.

About the team
This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to broaden their entertainment industry experience within Finance. Our team culture is goal orientated and fast paced. We are motivated by the challenge and success to produce premium and varied content in the ever-changing digital entertainment business.
Our team values analytical rigor, creative problem-solving, and collaborative partnerships to drive meaningful business outcomes. You'll join a supportive group of professionals who are passionate about leveraging financial expertise to influence business strategy in the entertainment industry.

jobs.description:
At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners. We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other. Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
As a Software Development Engineer, it’s up to you to define, design and refine the tech that keeps us one step ahead of listeners. Anticipate. Innovate. Bring challenging ideas and we’ll support you with the latest tech, tools, and systems that you need to succeed. We pride ourselves on our ability to imagine and invent solutions to problems of the future. You’ll be essential in this forward-thinking approach to development. Together with the team, you’ll build and maintain our platform to bring thousands of stories to millions of listeners and unleash the power of the human voice.

ABOUT YOU
If you’re a dedicated problem solver, this is the role for you. Identifying and overcoming challenges is at the heart of everything you do; whether it’s looking ahead to our listeners problems, or analyzing every inefficiency, you thrive when you strive for perfection. We look for people who take responsibility, who feel the pride in a job well done and see every failure as an opportunity to learn. With a knowledgeable team around you, and opportunities to challenge yourself, it’s a perfect environment to develop and apply your new skills.

As a Software Development Engineer, you will...
- Develop, test, and maintain software components and applications for the world's largest audiobook creator and provider
- Identify and resolve software defects through code review, log analysis, metrics examination, debugging, and other troubleshooting methods
- Write maintainable and scalable code for software components as part of an agile engineering team
- Collaborate with stakeholders to envision, design, develop, test, and launch customer-centric software that inspires and impacts users
- Apply computer science, engineering, and analysis principles to solve moderate-scale problems
- Continuously enhance testability, operational excellence (OE), and documentation for owned software components
- Handle data classification, storage, and management in accordance with company policies
- Participate in the interview process and provide mentorship to fellow engineers

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling. We spark listeners’ imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives. We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit. We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors. This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.
jobs.description_short: At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners.
